meta.title: Dominion SCC Data-Center Grid Intelligence (Virginia)
meta.what: Where data-center load can actually get powered in Dominion territory, distilled from Dominion's own Virginia SCC docket filings (1989-2026). Demand-side / energy dimension for DC Site Copilot.
meta.method: Semantic+keyword retrieval over a 1.1M-chunk local RAG of 68,000 Dominion SCC filings (mxbai-embed-large, cross-encoder rerank), 16 DC-siting queries, hand-synthesized with case+URL citations. RAG is local/free; teammates cannot re-query it, so this layer is pre-computed.
meta.confidence: Every MW/date/figure is sourced to a primary SCC case + document URL but tagged 'likely' until re-verified against the cited filing. Demo intelligence, not engineering advice.

tier_legend.saturated: Existing DC core, power-constrained, needs major new transmission, local zoning tightening. Hard to add new load.
tier_legend.building: Dominion has SCC-approved/active transmission + new DC substations underway. Proven-servable corridors — the migration whitespace.
tier_legend.emerging: Early load-area growth, new substation just filed; runway but less certainty.

statewide_demand.non_dc_load_trend: flat-to-declining through ~2029-2030; 100% of net load growth is data centers

sources.fact: 3,600 MW DC = ~20% of 18,409 MW peak, Dec 2024
load factor 63%->72% by 2039; non-DC load decoupled/declining
single campus 3,596 MW = twelve 300 MW substations (PEC IR 3-15)

large_load_queue.vs_dom_zone_peak: nearly triple the DOM Zone all-time peak
large_load_queue.process: Applies to DP Requests >=~100 MW serving data centers in DOM Zone; individual DP Requests capped at 300 MW each; 4 stages (Initiation, Feasibility, Development, Execution); submitted by the EDC via the Delivery Point Exchange (DPE), not the end customer; ESA-executed projects proposed to get queue priority (firmer, less speculative).

sources.fact: ~70,000 MW total large-load DP requests = nearly 3x DOM Zone peak; 25,000 MW assigned through 2031, 45,000 MW under study (Staff witness Joshipura)
9,000 MW signed ESAs, 6,000 MW CLOA, 26,000 MW under ELOA (Sierra Club, citing Company reporting Dec 2024)
queue process scope >=100 MW, 300 MW DP cap, 4 stages, DPE (Amazon witness Brooks)

rate_regime_gs5.summary: New GS-5 high-load-factor rate class, effective Jan 1, 2027 — the cost/risk regime a new VA data center signs into.
rate_regime_gs5.applies_to: customers with measured or contracted demand >= 25 MW AND load factor >= 75% (predominantly data centers); 139 current accounts
rate_regime_gs5.exit: may exit after 5 years (excluding ramp); exit fee = 36 months of minimum charges
rate_regime_gs5.collateral: 50% of total minimum charges for full contract term if below credit/liquidity thresholds
rate_regime_gs5.grandfathering: loads with signed LOA/ESA before the tariff effective date grandfathered unless they expand by >25 MW
rate_regime_gs5.purpose: protect against stranded costs + cost-shifting to non-DC ratepayers; discourage speculative overbuild

sources.fact: GS-5 criteria 25 MW & 75% LF, 139 accounts (Company witness Baine)
14-yr term, 85%/60% min demand, collateral, exit fees (PEC, post-approval)
exit after 5 yr = 36-mo exit fee; 50% collateral; $10k-$100k load study (Company witness Wishart)
14-yr term + ramp up to 4 yr (Amazon/joint stipulation)

flexibility_fast_track.summary: Flexible / phased grid connection + bring-your-own-capacity (BYOC) is the emerging way to power a DC faster than waiting for transmission.
flexibility_fast_track.avoided_per_gw: $78 million and 273 MW of capacity buildout per GW of DC demand (flexible connections)
mechanisms: conditional firm service (curtail during system stress)

sources.fact: flexible DCs connect 3-5 yr faster; avoid $78M + 273 MW/GW; 4 of 6 sites grid-constrained (Emerald AI / Princeton ZERO Lab)
phased/ramped connection brings DCs on faster within capacity limits (Sierra Club witness Nelson)

transmission_cost_signal.note: Dominion identified 88 planned transmission projects 'primarily being driven by data center load growth' (Sierra Club, citing Dominion Resource Plan). Network-upgrade cost is increasingly assigned to the large load that causes it (GS-5, CIAC, 'but-for' cost-cause tests).

load_areas.area: Data Center Alley (Eastern Loudoun Load Area)
load_areas.constraint: Power-constrained era since 2022; Board ended 'by-right' DC development March 18, 2025; relief requires a new 500 kV loop around DCA.
load_areas.dc_takeaway: Saturated + politically tightening. New large load faces transmission queue + community resistance. Power, not land, is the binding constraint.

load_areas.approved_load: ~30 million sq ft approved ≈ 4.5 GW; power must route through Loudoun
load_areas.constraint: Adds to the same constrained NoVA transmission corridor; rising community resistance.
load_areas.dc_takeaway: Big approved footprint but power delivery depends on the contested Loudoun 500 kV build. High execution risk.

load_areas.notes: Three DC campuses, 600 MW total; MEC (co-op) Visor DP +221 MW by 2035; resolves a 300 MW N-1-1 load-drop. The southern 'Data Center Alley'.
load_areas.dc_takeaway: Proven-servable Southside whitespace: Dominion is actively building 600 MW of DC substations here. Land cheaper, less NIMBY than NoVA.

load_areas.area: White Oak Technology Park (WOTP) / Technology Boulevard
new_substations: Saltwood Switching Station 230 kV (Development B, 300 MW)
load_areas.existing_dc: Portugee + White Oak Substations already serve Meta and QTS campuses
load_areas.transmission: Technology Boulevard 230 kV Project; lines rated 1,573 MVA summer
load_areas.dc_takeaway: Established central-VA DC node (Meta/QTS) actively expanding +600 MW on fresh 230 kV. Away from NoVA politics, near Richmond fiber.

load_areas.area: Western Chesterfield Load Area (Upper Magnolia Green)
new_substations: Amethyst (all 230 kV, for the planned DC development)
load_areas.constraint: Existing Midlothian/Winterpock substations are 34.5 kV and cannot support the 230 kV DC service; transformers saturate 2030-2037.
load_areas.dc_takeaway: ~1 GW DC build approaching by 2033 on brand-new 230 kV substations. Strong central-VA growth corridor south of Richmond.
262 MW DC on a new 230 kV substation on the I-95 corridor — exurban-NoVA growth with more land than Loudoun.

new_substations: West Creek 230 kV Loop and Substation
load_areas.notes: 37 MW for new manufacturing/warehouses plus future load; relieves Rockville, North Pole, Short Pump, River Road substations filling 2028-2030.
load_areas.dc_takeaway: Emerging central-VA frontier just west of Richmond/Short Pump; new 230 kV capacity arriving — early but real runway.

load_areas.notes: Built to serve a data-center customer; ties new DC load into the 500 kV Clover-Rawlings / Rawlings-Heritage backbone.
load_areas.dc_takeaway: Bulk 500/230 kV capacity arriving across deep Southside — the long-run expansion frontier as NoVA closes.
